title: Lomatogonium lloydioides
intro: Lomatogonium lloydioides, also known as Chinese Pimpernel, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lomatogonium lloydioides include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Lomatogonium lloydioides is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal soups and tinctures.
taxonomy: Lomatogonium lloydioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to China. The plant is classified within the genus Lomatogonium and is one of its many species. Lomatogonium lloydioides belongs to the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. It is closely related to other Gentianaceae species found in mountainous regions of Asia.
